title: Rootfull podman in rootless podman container says Using rootless single mapping into the namespace and fails to pull
body:
### Issue Description

I run a rootless podman container of `quay.io/podman/stable` without `--user` parameter and I try to `podman pull` in it.

Even if `id` in that container says that it is running as root, `podman pull` in that container says

```
Using rootless single mapping into the namespace. This might break some images. Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid for adding sub*ids if not using a network user
```
and fails with
```
Error: internal error: unable to copy from source docker://busybox:latest: copying system image from manifest list: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:90b9666d4aed1893ff122f238948dfd5e8efdcf6c444fe92371ea0f01750bf8c"/""/"sha256:65014c70e84b6817fac42bb201ec5c1ea460a8da246cac0e481f5c9a9491eac0": unpacking failed (error: exit status 1; output: potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace (requested 65534:65534 for /home): Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid if configured locally and run "podman system migrate": lchown /home: invalid argument)
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `host$ podman run --rm -ti quay.io/podman/stable`
2. `[root@2e2404b50845 /]# id`
3. `[root@2e2404b50845 /]# cat /proc/self/uid_map`
4. `[root@2e2404b50845 /]# touch /tmp/testfile ; chown 65534:65534 /tmp/testfile ; ls -ln /tmp/testfile`
5. `[root@2e2404b50845 /]# podman pull busybox`

### Describe the results you received

```
$ podman run --rm -ti quay.io/podman/stable
[root@2e2404b50845 /]# id
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)
[root@2e2404b50845 /]# cat /proc/self/uid_map
         0       1000          1
         1     100000      65536
[root@2e2404b50845 /]# touch /tmp/testfile ; chown 65534:65534 /tmp/testfile ; ls -ln /tmp/testfile
-rw-r--r--. 1 65534 65534 0 Jul 13 07:58 /tmp/testfile
[root@2e2404b50845 /]# podman pull busybox
WARN[0000] Using rootless single mapping into the namespace. This might break some images. Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid for adding sub*ids if not using a network user 
Resolved "busybox" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/busybox:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 90b9666d4aed done   | 
Error: internal error: unable to copy from source docker://busybox:latest: copying system image from manifest list: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:90b9666d4aed1893ff122f238948dfd5e8efdcf6c444fe92371ea0f01750bf8c"/""/"sha256:65014c70e84b6817fac42bb201ec5c1ea460a8da246cac0e481f5c9a9491eac0": unpacking failed (error: exit status 1; output: potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace (requested 65534:65534 for /home): Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid if configured locally and run "podman system migrate": lchown /home: invalid argument)
```

### Describe the results you expected

Image pulled without an error.

### podman info output

```yaml

  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.40.1
  cgroupControllers:
  - cpu
  - memory

    idlePercent: 89.44

  distribution:

    variant: xfce

  freeLocks: 2045
  hostname: machine.example.com
  idMappings:

    - container_id: 0

    uidmap:

    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 6.15.5-200.fc42.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  logDriver: journald
  memFree: 7111221248

    backend: netavark
    dns:
      package: aardvark-dns-1.15.0-1.fc42.x86_64
      path: /usr/libexec/podman/aardvark-dns
      version: aardvark-dns 1.15.0
    package: netavark-1.15.2-1.fc42.x86_64

      libseccomp: 2.5.5
  swapFree: 8589930496

  uptime: 17h 34m 40.00s (Approximately 0.71 days)
  variant: ""

  authorization: null
  log:
  - k8s-file
  - none
  - passthrough

  volume:

  search:
  - registry.fedoraproject.org

    Supports d_type: "true"

    number: 36

  volumePath: /var/lib/containers/test/storage/volumes

  APIVersion: 5.5.2
  BuildOrigin: Fedora Project
  Built: 1750723200
  BuiltTime: Tue Jun 24 02:00:00 2025
  GitCommit: e7d8226745ba07a64b7176a7f128e4ef53225a0e

  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 5.5.2
```

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

When I run the rootless container with `--user podman`, the `podman pull` in the container passes:
```
$ podman run --rm -ti --user podman quay.io/podman/stable podman pull busybox
Resolved "busybox" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/busybox:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 90b9666d4aed done   | 
Copying config 6d3e4188a3 done   | 
Writing manifest to image destination
6d3e4188a38af91b0c1577b9e88c53368926b2fe0e1fb985d6e8a70040520c4d
```

Seeing that in my original container which from the point of view of the podman in it should act as a rootfull one (shouldn't it?) I get that message about rootless single mapping into the namespace, I even tried to define the subuid / subgid for root:
```
[root@2e2404b50845 /]# echo root:1:100000 >> /etc/subuid
[root@2e2404b50845 /]# echo root:1:100000 >> /etc/subgid
```

That does get rid of the warning message about the rootless single mapping into the namespace but the result of `podman pull` is the same.

```
[root@2e2404b50845 /]# podman pull busybox
Resolved "busybox" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/busybox:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 90b9666d4aed done   | 
Error: internal error: unable to copy from source docker://busybox:latest: copying system image from manifest list: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:90b9666d4aed1893ff122f238948dfd5e8efdcf6c444fe92371ea0f01750bf8c"/""/"sha256:65014c70e84b6817fac42bb201ec5c1ea460a8da246cac0e481f5c9a9491eac0": unpacking failed (error: exit status 1; output: potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace (requested 65534:65534 for /home): Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid if configured locally and run "podman system migrate": lchown /home: invalid argument)
```

comments.body:
I tried out your reproducer on Fedora 42 (amd64) but I don't get the same results. 

The command ```podman pull busybox``` succeeds.

(I created a new user `test11` for the test)
```
root@asus:~# useradd test11
root@asus:~# machinectl shell --uid=test11
Connected to the local host. Press ^] three times within 1s to exit session.
test11@asus:~$ podman run --rm -ti quay.io/podman/stable
Trying to pull quay.io/podman/stable:latest...
Getting image source signatures

uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)
[root@1621673c6876 /]# cat /proc/self/uid_map
         0       1012          1
         1    1245184      65536

-rw-r--r--. 1 65534 65534 0 Jul 13 09:43 /tmp/testfile
[root@1621673c6876 /]# podman pull busybox

exit
test11@asus:~$ 
```

I see some differences, for example

```
-  databaseBackend: boltdb
```
vs
```
+  databaseBackend: sqlite
```

```
-  volumePath: /var/lib/containers/test/storage/volumes
```
vs
```
+  volumePath: /home/test11/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
```

can't reproduce locally.

Since it runs with uid != 0, the newuidmap, newgidmap tools should have the correct capabilities, but they are not used when running as root, as we can simply copy the mappings.

What is the podman version inside the image?  What capabilities does the container have?

This is what I've:

```
➜  podman run --rm -ti quay.io/podman/stable sh -c 'podman --version; getpcaps 1'
podman version 5.5.2
1: cap_chown,cap_dac_override,cap_fowner,cap_fsetid,cap_kill,cap_setgid,cap_setuid,cap_setpcap,cap_net_bind_service,cap_sys_chroot,cap_setfcap=ep
```

Thanks for checking it on your setups. I confirm that with a fresh Fedora 42 VM, I don't get the error either.

I will try to figure out what is the ultimate difference between my base system and the VM.
Hmm, and now I'm not even able to reproduce the issue on the original system. Sorry about the noise.
